candidates.reasoning: The combination of limestone-rich, light-colored rocky soil and Mediterranean pine forests (Pinus halepensis/brutia) is highly characteristic of the Greek highlands. The arid, scrubby undergrowth and road construction style are typical for rural mountainous regions in Greece.

candidates.reasoning: The landscape is dominated by Pinus halepensis (Aleppo pine) and Pinus brutia (Turkish pine) on rocky, calcareous soil, which is highly characteristic of the Greek mainland and islands. This specific combination of pine species and arid, scrubby undergrowth is a hallmark of the Mediterranean basin, particularly in Greece.

The vegetation is nearly identical to that of Greece, as Pinus brutia is native to the Aegean and Mediterranean coasts of Turkey. The rocky terrain and pine distribution are consistent with the Taurus mountains or the Aegean coast, making it botanically indistinguishable from Greece in this view.

The presence of Pinus halepensis is common in eastern and southern Spain. However, the specific growth habit and soil color are slightly more characteristic of the Eastern Mediterranean (Greece/Turkey) than the Iberian Peninsula.

The road quality, lack of center lines on rural mountain roads, and the specific red-and-white reflective delineator post on the right shoulder are highly characteristic of Turkish rural infrastructure. This style of delineator is common across the Mediterranean region but matches Turkish standards closely.

The infrastructure is very similar to Turkey, featuring similar rural road standards and red-and-white delineator posts. Without visible signage or license plates, it is difficult to distinguish from Turkey based solely on road markings.
Spain uses similar red-and-white delineator posts on rural roads, though the general road surface and shoulder composition are slightly more typical of the Eastern Mediterranean.

candidates.reasoning: The red and white delineator post on the right side of the road is highly characteristic of Turkey. While similar posts exist in other Mediterranean countries, the specific shape and reflector placement are common in Turkish rural mountain roads.

Greece uses similar red and white delineator posts in mountainous regions, though the specific design often differs slightly from the Turkish standard. The overall road quality and infrastructure style are consistent with the region.
